sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to be refractory, stubborn; pr. used of refractory beasts (kindred root סוּר). Only in the part. [except Hos 4:16 ] סֹרֵר f. סֹרֶרֶת refractory, stubborn; used of an untamed cow, Hos 4:16 of a son who refuses to submit to his parents, Deu 21:18, 20 Deu 21:20 Psa 78:8 Jer 5:23 of a woman who has cast off restraint and indulges in lusts Pro 7:11 of the people of Israel, Isa 1:23 30:1 65:2 Psa 68:19 Hos 9:15. From the idea of stubborn animals shaking the yoke off from their shoulders, is taken the phrase נָתַן כָּתֵף סֹרֶרֶת to give a refractory shoulder, i.e. to be stubborn, Neh 9:29 Zec 7:11. There is a paronomasia in Jer 6:28, סָרֵי סֹרְרִים “those fallen away to the stubborn.”

(2) to be evil, i.q. Arab. شَرَّ. Hence סַר.
